values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

This looks reasonable.  Is there some way that we could test this is working correctly?  Maybe a test in replay-single which restarts the MDS, then checks the RPC stats to see that < 8 LDLM RPCs in flight when recovery has finished?  It might be enough to add this to an existing test that has a bunch of MDS locks outstanding.

Patch Set 2:

I was thinking about test but rpc stats doesn't include recovery requests it seems, so a new stat variable is needed if we want to track this

Patch Set 2:

> I was thinking about test but rpc stats doesn't include recovery
 > requests it seems, so a new stat variable is needed if we want to
 > track this

Since this is a patch changing the LDLM code, it would definitely also be possible to add a stats counter for the lock replays, if that made writing a test easier.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 3b613a442b8698596096b23ce82e157c158a5874 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-13580 tests: fix retrieval of SELinux context

commit.subject: LU-13600 ptlrpc: limit rate of lock replays
commit.message:
LU-13600 ptlrpc: limit rate of lock replays

Clients send all lock replays at once and that may overwhelm
server with huge amount of replays in recovery queue causing
OOM effects.

Patch adds rate control for lock replays on client

Signed-off-by: Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ie557f8481c5facb690468d7136cf5feebe4e8f11
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/38920
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
